reasoning: Google Chrome holds a huge, undisputed lead in global web browser market share, currently commanding around 64% across all devices compared to its nearest competitor (Safari at ~19%). Browser market shares exhibit strong inertia and change very slowly over time due to established user habits, enterprise defaults, and deep integration with dominant operating systems like Android. Even accounting for regulatory scrutiny (such as forced choice screens in the EU) or technological shifts (such as the integration of generative AI into competitors like Edge), it is highly implausible that a ~45% market share gap could be entirely erased by the end of 2025. Therefore, it is virtually certain Chrome will remain the most popular browser.
